calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Affirmed

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award As Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIMOTHY AIRES APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. SONIA Y. LEE OF THE RAINES DELDMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-310-440-4100 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Broadcast Music Inc.'s petition to confirm arbitration award is denied and respondent Golden State Broadcasting LLC's cross-motion to vacate the arbitration award is granted. The Stolz declaration demonstrates that BMI served the demand for arbitration and subsequent documents on Golden State at the wrong or outdated address and that Golden State was unaware of the arbitration in time to defend the claim made by BMI in the arbitration. The FCC ownership report refers to the Fred Waring Drive address. That report indicates that the Fred Waring Drive address was accurate as of October 1, 2013 or December 13, but the demand for arbitration and service of award occurred approximately 2 years later. BMI should have served Golden State at the Executive Park Boulevard or West Tropicana Boulevard address. When BMI brought a federal copyright infringement action against Golden State, the summons identified the West Tropicana Boulevard address and Golden State responded to that action. The motion to vacate is timely. "A petition to vacate an award or to correct an award shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award on the petitioner." (CCP 1288). The 100-day period has yet to run because there is no showing that the award was properly served on Golden State. The award is vacated per CCP 1286.2(a)(1) because it "was procured by'other undue means" because Golden State lacked notice of the arbitration demand and arbitration proceedings and was unable to respond. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award As Judgment

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER BROADCAST MUSIC INC's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award As Judgment is CONTINUED to January 9, 2017, to give the parties a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.7B. All parties are ordered to immediately provide courtesy copies of their filings with Dept. 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru
